_meta.description: Canonical features manifest for Stellar Broadcast. Audited from source code and cross-referenced with website/press kit claims.

game.name: Stellar Broadcast
game.subtitle: Humanity's Last Voyage
game.genre: Roguelike narrative sci-fi / colony ship survival

features.name: Branching narrative events
features.description: 140 GameEvent objects across 6 categories with multiple choices and consequences
features.discrepancy: Website says 100+, actual count is 140. Website is accurate (understating).

features.name: Ship systems degradation
features.description: 14 total systems (6 core + 1 defense + 1 landing + 6 scanners) that degrade during voyage
features.discrepancy: Website says 8 (likely counting core + shields + landing = 8 non-scanner systems). Full count including scanners is 14.

features.name: Interactive alien puzzles
features.description: 6 puzzle types (pi digits, fibonacci, primes, spectral identification, star cluster, chirality) x 3 alien species
features.websiteClaim: spectral analysis, constellation mapping, crystalline decoding
features.discrepancy: Press kit lists 3 puzzle types; actual code has 6 types x 3 species = 18 combinations.

features.name: Procedural planets
features.description: 10 planet stats, 42 surface features, 5 moon types, 4 ring types, native civilizations with disposition
features.websiteClaim: Procedural planets with moons, rings, native civilizations, government structures

features.name: Neural network planet naming
features.description: LSTM model generating planet names conditioned on planet stats, running on-device via ONNX Runtime FP16
features.websiteClaim: Planet names generated by on-device LSTM neural network (ONNX Runtime, FP16)

features.name: Multiple ending tiers
features.description: 6 tiers from Golden Age (95+) to Extinction (0-14), max score 130, with 11 sub-score categories
features.websiteClaim: Multiple ending tiers from Utopia to Extinction
features.discrepancy: Press kit says 'Utopia to Extinction' but actual tier names are Golden Age, Thriving Colony, Survival, Struggling, Dire, Extinction. No tier is named 'Utopia'.

features.name: Persistent legacy system
features.description: 8 permanent upgrades purchasable with legacy points, plus voyage logs and high score tracking
features.websiteClaim: Persistent legacy system with permanent upgrades between runs

features.name: Daily challenge mode
features.description: Daily Voyage with shared seed, separate daily scores, dedicated leaderboard
features.websiteClaim: Daily challenge mode with shared seeds and Google Play leaderboards

features.name: Google Play Games integration
features.description: 3 leaderboards (best score, daily, encounters) and 10 achievements

features.name: Localization
features.description: 20 .arb language files including zh_TW as a separate locale

features.name: Ambient soundtrack
features.description: Audio via audioplayers + flame_audio packages, assets/audio/ directory
features.websiteClaim: Original ambient soundtrack with reactive sound effects

features.name: Special visual event screens
features.description: 7 dedicated animated screens for rare encounters (black hole, chrono vortex, dyson sphere, living nebula, mirror array, seed vault, world engine)
features.discrepancy: Website does not highlight these 7 special visual events. Could be a marketing opportunity.

features.name: Trader encounters
features.description: Dedicated trader screen with trading mechanics, triggered by events with openTraderScreen flag
features.discrepancy: Trading system exists but is not mentioned on website or press kit.

features.name: Codex/encyclopedia
features.description: Codex screen tracking discovered features across voyages
features.discrepancy: Codex feature exists but is not mentioned on website or press kit.

features.name: Seed codes & voyage logs
features.description: 6-char alphanumeric seed codes, full voyage log entries stored in legacy data
features.websiteClaim: Share seed codes to challenge friends on the exact same galaxy

features.name: Responsive tablet/landscape layouts
features.description: 14 screens with dedicated landscape layouts, 7 additional screens with responsive content wrappers
features.discrepancy: Responsive tablet support is not advertised anywhere.

features.name: In-app purchases / Premium
features.description: Premium paywall screen exists, monthly ($2.99) and lifetime ($5.99) options
features.discrepancy: Press kit only mentions $5.99 one-time; code also has $2.99 monthly option.

features.name: Moon landing choice
features.description: Players can choose to land on habitable moons instead of the planet if the moon is more habitable
features.discrepancy: Moon landing mechanic exists but is not advertised.

features.name: Colony naming
features.description: Players can name their colony at landing, shown in ending and voyage logs

features.name: Government types in endings
features.description: Colony government type generated based on ship state and planet at landing
features.websiteClaim: government structures mentioned in planet description

websiteVsCodeDiscrepancies.note: Website understates. Update to '140+' or '135+' for headroom.
Website counts 8 non-scanner systems. Could say '14 systems' or '8 core systems + 6 scanners' for accuracy.

websiteVsCodeDiscrepancies.website: spectral analysis, constellation mapping, crystalline decoding
websiteVsCodeDiscrepancies.actual: 6 types: pi digits, fibonacci, primes, spectral ID, star cluster, chirality
websiteVsCodeDiscrepancies.note: Press kit undersells puzzle variety. 6 distinct puzzle types exist across 3 alien species.

websiteVsCodeDiscrepancies.actual: Golden Age, Thriving Colony, Survival, Struggling, Dire, Extinction
websiteVsCodeDiscrepancies.note: Press kit says 'Utopia' but the top tier is actually 'Golden Age'. Minor naming mismatch.
Monthly subscription option not mentioned in press kit.

websiteVsCodeDiscrepancies.actual: 7 dedicated animated screens (black hole, chrono vortex, dyson sphere, living nebula, mirror array, seed vault, world engine)
websiteVsCodeDiscrepancies.note: Major marketing opportunity. These are visually impressive and differentiating.
Could be mentioned as a feature.
Could be mentioned as a feature for completionists.

websiteVsCodeDiscrepancies.actual: 14 screens with landscape tablet layouts + 7 with responsive wrappers
websiteVsCodeDiscrepancies.note: Tablet support could be mentioned for Play Store listing (large screen devices).

websiteVsCodeDiscrepancies.actual: Can land on habitable moons if more habitable than planet

websiteVsCodeDiscrepancies.note: Could add '40+ surface features' to reinforce procedural depth.
JSON-LD on website lists 19 language codes but claims 20 languages. Missing zh_TW in the array.
